questions.prompt:
A student investigates how oxygen availability affects yeast cells. Two sealed flasks contain the same amount of yeast and glucose solution. Flask A has oxygen pumped into it before sealing. Flask B has no added oxygen. After 20 minutes, the student measures the amount of ethanol produced.
Flask | Oxygen added? | Ethanol produced (mL)
A | Yes | 1
B | No | 9
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Yeast produced more ethanol when oxygen was limited.
choices.B: Yeast produced more ethanol when oxygen was added.
choices.C: Oxygen level had no relationship to ethanol production.
choices.D: Yeast cannot use glucose in any condition.

questions.correctAnswerText: Yeast produced more ethanol when oxygen was limited.
questions.explanation: Flask B had no added oxygen and produced 9 mL of ethanol, more than Flask A.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the speed of a cart at different times.
Time (s): 0, 2, 4, 6
Speed (m/s): 1, 5, 9, 13
What is the cart’s average acceleration from 0 to 6 seconds?
questions.explanation: Speed increased from 1 m/s to 13 m/s, a change of 12 m/s over 6 seconds. Acceleration = 12 ÷ 6 = 2 m/s².

questions.prompt:
A scientist studies two nearby areas. Area A is covered by natural grassland. Area B has been converted into bare, compacted soil by repeated vehicle traffic. During heavy rain, Area B has more surface runoff and less water entering the soil than Area A.
Which explanation best fits the observations?

choices.A: Compacted soil has fewer connected pore spaces, so less water infiltrates.
choices.B: Compacted soil creates new rain during storms.
choices.C: Grassland prevents gravity from acting on water.
choices.D: Bare soil always absorbs more water than soil with plants.

questions.correctAnswerText: Compacted soil has fewer connected pore spaces, so less water infiltrates.
questions.explanation: Compaction reduces pore space and permeability, increasing runoff and reducing infiltration.

questions.prompt:
During intense exercise, muscle cells may need energy faster than oxygen can be delivered. The cells can temporarily use fermentation to release some energy from glucose. This process produces lactic acid, which can build up in muscles.
Which statement best describes fermentation in muscle cells?

choices.A: It can release some energy when oxygen supply is limited.
choices.C: It removes all glucose from the body permanently.
choices.D: It happens only in bones and teeth.

questions.correctAnswerText: It can release some energy when oxygen supply is limited.
questions.explanation: Fermentation allows cells to release some energy from glucose when oxygen is limited.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A motorbike increases its speed from 4 m/s to 22 m/s in 6 seconds.
Acceleration = change in speed ÷ time
What is the motorbike’s acceleration in m/s²?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Change in speed = 22 − 4 = 18 m/s. Acceleration = 18 ÷ 6 = 3 m/s².

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how ramp angle affects the final speed of a ball. The student uses the same ball and releases it from the same starting point on ramps set at different angles.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?
questions.explanation: These factors should stay the same so ramp angle is the main variable being tested.

questions.prompt: A person with Down syndrome usually has three copies of chromosome 21 instead of two. This condition results from a change in [D1], not from a change in the sequence of a single gene. The extra chromosome can be identified with [D2].
questions.explanation: Down syndrome usually involves an extra chromosome 21, which can be identified by a karyotype.

questions.prompt:
A spoon appears bent when placed partly in a glass of water. Light travels at different speeds in air and water, so its direction changes when it passes from one material into the other.
Which wave behavior explains the apparent bending?
questions.explanation: Refraction occurs when light changes direction as it passes between materials.

questions.prompt:
A text model describes Earth’s layers. The crust is the thin outer layer. Below it, the mantle contains hot rock that can slowly flow over long periods. The outer core is liquid metal, and the inner core is solid metal under very high pressure.
Which layer is described as liquid metal?
questions.explanation: Earth’s outer core is described as liquid metal.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A researcher records the number of people reporting pollen allergy symptoms during four weeks.
Week | Average pollen level | People reporting symptoms
1 | Low | 12
2 | Moderate | 31
3 | High | 58
4 | Very high | 74
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Reports of allergy symptoms increased as pollen level increased.
choices.B: Reports of allergy symptoms decreased as pollen level increased.
choices.C: Pollen level had no relationship to symptoms.
choices.D: The highest symptom count occurred when pollen level was low.

questions.correctAnswerText: Reports of allergy symptoms increased as pollen level increased.
questions.explanation: The number of people reporting symptoms rose from 12 to 74 as pollen level increased.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.B: Distance at which the image is focused
tiles.C: Same object placed in front of each lens
tiles.D: Brand of notebook used for recording

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Type of lens used; T2=Distance at which the image is focused; T3=Same object placed in front of each lens
questions.explanation: The lens type is changed, focus distance is measured, and the object is controlled.

questions.prompt:
The immune system responds to a small cut when damaged cells release chemical signals. White blood cells move toward the area, and blood vessels nearby may widen. The area can become red, warm, and swollen as the body begins repair and defense.
Which response is mainly described?
questions.explanation: Redness, warmth, swelling, and white blood cell movement are signs of inflammation.

questions.prompt:
A sealed balloon is placed in a warm room. The gas particles inside the balloon move faster and collide with the balloon walls more often and more forcefully. The balloon expands slightly.
Which statement best explains the change?

choices.A: Heating the gas increased particle motion and pressure, causing expansion.
choices.B: Heating the gas removed all particles from the balloon.
choices.C: Heating the gas turned it into solid metal.
choices.D: Heating the gas stopped all collisions.

questions.correctAnswerText: Heating the gas increased particle motion and pressure, causing expansion.
questions.explanation: Warmer gas particles move faster and collide more forcefully with the balloon walls.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A satellite completes 12 orbits around Earth in 36 hours.
What is the average time for one orbit in hours?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 36 hours ÷ 12 orbits = 3 hours per orbit.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether a certain soil fungus affects tomato root growth. One group of tomato plants is grown in soil with the fungus. A second group is grown in the same type of soil without the fungus. All plants receive the same light, water, and pot size. After four weeks, root length is measured.
Why is the group without the fungus included?

choices.A: It provides a comparison for the fungus-treated group.
choices.B: It guarantees that roots will not grow.
choices.C: It changes the fungus into plant tissue.
choices.D: It eliminates the need to measure root length.

questions.correctAnswerText: It provides a comparison for the fungus-treated group.
questions.explanation: The group without fungus acts as a comparison group to judge the effect of the fungus.

questions.prompt:
A student compares how quickly different metals conduct heat. Rods of copper, steel, and brass are placed with one end in hot water. A small wax bead is attached to the other end of each rod. The student records the time until each wax bead melts.
What is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Time until the wax bead melts

questions.correctAnswerText: Time until the wax bead melts
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured outcome after changing the type of metal.

questions.prompt: When two magnets are brought near each other, opposite poles [D1], while like poles [D2].
questions.explanation: Opposite magnetic poles attract, while like magnetic poles repel.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A lab technician tracks oxygen use by cells at different temperatures.
Temperature (°C): 15, 25, 35, 45
Oxygen used in 10 min (mL): 6, 14, 29, 13
Which temperature was associated with the highest oxygen use?
questions.explanation: The highest oxygen use, 29 mL, occurred at 35°C.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the input force needed to lift the same load using different pulley setups.
Pulley setup | Input force needed (N)
A | 120
B | 80
C | 60
D | 40
Which setup gives the greatest mechanical advantage?
questions.explanation: For the same load, the setup requiring the least input force has the greatest mechanical advantage.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A city compares two transportation patterns and carbon dioxide emissions.
Year | Public transit trips (millions) | Transportation CO₂ emissions (thousand tons)
1 | 24 | 910
2 | 31 | 850
3 | 39 | 790
4 | 46 | 730
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Public transit trips increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.B: Transportation CO₂ emissions decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.C: CO₂ emissions increased as public transit trips increased.
choices.D: Public transit trips stayed the same every year.
choices.E: Year 4 had the highest transportation CO₂ emissions.

questions.correctAnswerText: Public transit trips increased from Year 1 to Year 4. | Transportation CO₂ emissions decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
questions.explanation: Transit trips rose from 24 million to 46 million, while emissions decreased from 910 to 730 thousand tons.

questions.prompt:
A person has a damaged heart valve that does not close completely. Some blood leaks backward each time the heart pumps. As a result, less blood may move forward to deliver oxygen and nutrients to body tissues.
Which function is most directly affected?

choices.A: Circulation of blood through the body
choices.B: Breakdown of starch in the mouth
choices.C: Exchange of genetic information during meiosis

questions.correctAnswerText: Circulation of blood through the body
questions.explanation: A damaged valve can reduce the heart’s ability to move blood forward efficiently.

questions.prompt:
A student observes water boiling in an open pot. The temperature of the water remains near 100°C while boiling continues. Added energy is used to change liquid water into water vapor instead of raising the temperature.
Which statement best explains the constant temperature during boiling?

choices.A: Added energy is used for a phase change.
choices.B: Added energy disappears and is not transferred.
choices.D: The water becomes colder while boiling.

questions.correctAnswerText: Added energy is used for a phase change.
questions.explanation: During boiling, added energy changes liquid water into vapor instead of increasing temperature.

questions.prompt:
A volcano releases ash and sulfur dioxide high into the atmosphere during a large eruption. Some particles and gases can reflect sunlight back to space for a short time, reducing the amount of solar energy reaching Earth’s surface.
Which short-term effect could result from a large eruption?

choices.D: Immediate formation of all fossil fuels

questions.explanation: Ash and sulfur compounds can reflect sunlight and temporarily reduce surface heating.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A survey of 320 students finds that 88 students report having asthma.
What percent of the students report having asthma?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student wants to test how blade angle affects the lift produced by a small model propeller. The student changes the blade angle but keeps the motor speed and blade length the same. The lift force is measured for each angle.
Which factor is the independent variable?
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt:
A plant cell has a large central vacuole filled with water and dissolved substances. When the vacuole is full, it presses against the cell wall and helps keep the plant upright.
Which statement best describes one function of the central vacuole?

choices.A: It helps maintain pressure that supports the plant cell.
choices.B: It pumps blood through plant veins.
choices.C: It controls the orbit of planets.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps maintain pressure that supports the plant cell.
questions.explanation: A full central vacuole creates pressure against the cell wall, helping support the plant cell.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures electrical energy used by four devices in the same amount of time.
Device | Voltage (V) | Current (A)
A | 12 | 1
B | 12 | 2
C | 12 | 3
D | 12 | 4
Power = voltage × current
Which device has the greatest power?
questions.explanation: Power = voltage × current. Device D has 12 V × 4 A = 48 W, the greatest value.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records the percentage of land covered by forest in a region.
Year | Forest cover (%)
1990 | 64
2000 | 58
2010 | 51
2020 | 43
What was the change in forest cover from 1990 to 2020?
questions.explanation: Forest cover decreased from 64% to 43%, a change of 21 percentage points.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A lab compares the average time needed for blood to clot in four samples.
Sample | Clotting time (seconds)
A | 42
B | 65
C | 38
D | 51
Which sample clotted fastest?
questions.explanation: The fastest clotting sample has the shortest clotting time, 38 seconds.

questions.prompt:
A student places a copper wire in a blue solution containing silver ions. After some time, silver-colored crystals form on the wire and the solution changes color. Atoms and ions have rearranged to form new substances.
Which type of change occurred?
questions.explanation: New silver-colored crystals and solution color change indicate new substances formed.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A researcher studies whether different salt levels affect bean seedling root growth. Seeds are placed in solutions with low, medium, or high salt levels. The researcher measures root length after five days.
Which TWO steps would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same type of bean seed in all groups.
choices.B: Use the same number of seeds at each salt level.
choices.C: Change light level and salt level at the same time.
choices.D: Measure only the seedling with the longest root.
choices.E: Use a different growth time for each group.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same type of bean seed in all groups. | Use the same number of seeds at each salt level.
questions.explanation: Keeping seed type and sample size constant improves the investigation.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the distance traveled by a sound pulse in a material.
Time (s): 1, 2, 3, 4
Distance (m): 1,500, 3,000, 4,500, 6,000
What is the speed of the sound pulse in this material?
questions.explanation: Speed = distance ÷ time. The sound pulse travels 1,500 meters each second.

questions.prompt:
In a pond, leeches attach to fish and feed on their blood. The leeches benefit by getting food, while the fish may lose energy and become more likely to develop infections.
Which type of relationship is described?
questions.explanation: The leech benefits while the fish is harmed, which is parasitism.

questions.prompt:
Some antibiotics interfere with bacterial ribosomes. Human cells have ribosomes too, but bacterial ribosomes are different enough that certain antibiotics can affect bacteria more strongly than human cells.
What is the main reason these antibiotics can target bacteria?

choices.A: Bacterial ribosomes differ from human ribosomes.
choices.B: Bacteria do not contain any genetic material.
choices.C: Human cells do not make proteins.

questions.correctAnswerText: Bacterial ribosomes differ from human ribosomes.
questions.explanation: Some antibiotics affect bacterial ribosomes because they are different from human ribosomes.
